title: envelope in elsif test bypasses MissingRequire check
description: TRIGGER: compile(b"if true { keep; } elsif envelope :is \"from\" \"x\" { discard; }") without require [\"envelope\"]. BAD BEHAVIOR: returns Ok instead of MissingRequire(\"envelope\"). ROOT CAUSE: check_extension_use scans test forms only up to the FIRST Block in the if-chain. The elsif test forms appear after Block([[keep]]) in the flat Stmt and are never scanned for extension test names. The bottom for-loop only recurses into Form::Block and Form::TestList items, not bare Word items in the middle of the stmt. All extension tests that appear as direct (non-TestList) tests in elsif clauses bypass the check. Confirmed empirically: is_err=false when it should be true.
